createairwork.ponder.navigator: Autopilot control center for your ship
createairwork.ponder.navigator.text_1: The Navigator is the autopilot control center for your ship
createairwork.ponder.navigator.text_2: It requires at least one Rocket Engine on the ship
createairwork.ponder.navigator.text_3: ...and at least one Gyroscope for stabilization
createairwork.ponder.navigator.text_4: Right-click to open the navigation interface
createairwork.ponder.navigator.text_5: Set target coordinates and the ship will fly there automatically!
createairwork.ponder.navigator.text_6: The Navigator must be placed on a VS2 ship to function
createairwork.ponder.rocket_data_link: Control multiple rocket engines from one place
createairwork.ponder.rocket_data_link.te: The Rocket Engine provides thrust for Valkyrien Skies ships
Place a Rocket Data Link nearby to control multiple engines
Right-click the Data Link to receive the linking tool
Right-click engines to link them to the Data Link
Open the Data Link interface to start or stop all linked engines
Redstone signal to Data Link controls ALL linked engines!
Signal strength controls thrust of ALL engines at once!
Monitor fuel levels and engine status from the interface!
createairwork.ponder.rocket_engine: Powerful thrust for your Valkyrien Skies ships
createairwork.ponder.rocket_engine.text_: The Rocket Engine creates thrust for Valkyrien Skies ships
Use a Mechanical Pump to feed Rocket Fuel from behind
Right-click to manually start the engine
Redstone signal activates thrust when engine is running
Wear Engineer's Goggles to see status and fuel level
Use Rocket Data Link to control multiple engines at once!
createairwork.rocket_data_link.different: Engine must be on the same ship!
